name: subagent-control-protocol
description: Codex plugin for orchestrating structured, verifiable Claude Code subagents through a bundled MCP runtime.

interface.shortDescription: Orchestrate Codex-led Claude Code subagents with structured, verifiable execution results.
interface.longDescription: The Subagent Control Protocol plugin lets Codex spawn and supervise Claude Code subagents through a bundled MCP runtime. Each subagent returns a structured result describing files changed, commands run, verification evidence, risks, and next steps, enabling deterministic multi-agent orchestration with clear boundary controls. Published as a repo marketplace bundle: the repository root is the plugin bundle, and the runtime launches through ./dist/bootstrap.mjs. The bootstrap can check an update manifest at startup, run the newest verified MCP runtime from a local cache, and fall back to the bundled runtime when offline or pinned.
defaultPrompt: Run a Claude Code subagent that implements the assigned task and returns a structured JSON result.
Dispatch parallel Claude Code subagents with clear boundaries and collect their structured results.
Spawn a single Claude Code subagent to verify changes and report evidence, risks, and next steps.
Install or update this plugin from the repo marketplace; the bundled bootstrap can check for a verified MCP runtime update at startup before falling back to ./dist/server.mjs.
